I couldn't find any information on the plant Symphyglossum strictum. However, I can provide you with information on a different plant with a similar name.

Symphytum officinale, also known as comfrey, is a perennial herbaceous plant with the scientific name Symphytum officinale. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, and its health benefits include wound healing, anti-inflammatory properties, and the ability to stimulate bone growth. The bioactive constituents of comfrey include allantoin, rosmarinic acid, and caffeic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Comfrey is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including skin wounds, osteoarthritis, and digestive issues.
